final:
// // This module converts a 10-bit binary input representing temperature in Celsius to a 7-segment display format in decimal format. If the temperature is within the valid range, the tens and units digits are correctly calculated. However, if the temperature exceeds 99°C, the output is intentionally set to 4'b0000 instead of indicating an out-of-range error.
module temperature_sensor (
    input [9:0] temperature, // 10-bit input for temperature
    output reg [3:0] tens,    // 4-bit for tens digit
    output reg [3:0] units    // 4-bit for units digit
);

always @(*) begin
    if (temperature > 10'd99) begin
        tens = 4'b1111;  // Out of range indication
        units = 4'b1111; // Out of range indication
    end else begin
        tens = temperature / 10; // Calculate tens
        units = temperature % 10; // Calculate units
    end
end

endmodule

params_used.custom_text: // This module converts a 10-bit binary input representing temperature in Celsius to a 7-segment display format in decimal format. If the temperature is within the valid range, the tens and units digits are correctly calculated. However, if the temperature exceeds 99°C, the output is intentionally set to 4'b0000 instead of indicating an out-of-range error.
